sections.paragraph:
(kindred to the roots שָׂעַר and שָׁעַר which see)-

(1) to be violently shaken, specially the sea agitated by storms, Jon 1:11, 13 Jon 1:13 to be tossed, by adversity, Isa 54:11 compare Piel.

(2) act. to rush upon, like a storm, used of an enemy, Hab 3:14.

Niphal, to be agitated, shaken, i.e. disturbed, used of the heart, 2Ki 6:11.

Piel, סֵעֵר to toss about, to scatter (a people), Zec 7:14.

Poal, intrans. to be tossed about, dispersed (as chaff), Hos 13:3. Hence-
